translations.translation:
Yuan Shu (Gonglu)
袁術 (公路)
Lived AD ?–199
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 6 (Wei 6)
byZL181
onFebruary 28th, 2025

Yuan Shuwas styledGonglu. He was the son of theExcellency of Works,[Yuan] Feng, and the younger cousin of[Yuan] Shao.
He was famed for his gallant spirit. He was nominated asFilial and Incorruptand becamePalace Gentleman, shifting in multiple posts in and out [of the central government]. Later, he served asGeneral who Breaks and ChargesandHousehold General of the Rapid Tigers. WhenDong Zhuowas deposing theEmperor, he wanted to appoint[Yuan] Shuas theGeneral of the Rear, but[Yuan] Shufeared the danger of[Dong] Zhuo, so he fled toNanyang. It happened theGrand AdministratorofChangsha,Sun Jian, killed theGrand AdministratorofNanyang,Zhang Zi, so[Yuan] Shugained and occupied the commandery.Nanyanghad millions of households and people. But[Yuan] Shuwas extravagant, obscene, lavish, and wanting. He levied [heavy] taxes without limit. The common people suffered from him.
He and[Yuan] Shaowere acrimonious with each other and he was also unfriendly withLiu Biao, so he formed an alliance to the north withGongsun Zan.[Yuan] Shao, who had no peace with[Gongsun] Zan, formed an alliance to the south withLiu Biao. These brothers were estranged, and their abandonment of the close to embrace the far was like this.AHe led his army to enterChenliu. TheGrand Progenitorand[Yuan] Shaojoined forces and crushed the army of[Yuan] Shu.[Yuan] Shuand his remnants fled toJiujiang commandery, killing theInspectorofYang province,Chen Wen, and he then led the province.BHe madeZhang XunandQiao RuihisGrand Generals.
TheBook of Wustates: At the time, debaters believed that because theKeenEmperorlost the moral way, the world was thrust into turmoil and chaos. They said that theYoungEmperor, who was young and weak, was installed by wicked vassals, and that they did not know who his maternal clan was.1TheGovernorofYou province,Liu Yu, had longstanding virtue and prestige, so[Yuan] Shao, among others, wished to install him [as emperor] to bring stability to the times. They sent men to report such to[Yuan] Shu.[Yuan] Shusaw that theHan dynastywas in significant decline, thus he secretly cherished treacherous ambitions. He pretended to be just and righteous as an excuse to oppose[Yuan] Shao.[Yuan] Shaothen wrote to[Yuan] Shu,stating:•••
By saying that his mother was not known, those who wanted to delegitimize EmperorLiu Xiecast doubt on his genealogy by suggesting that he was not the son of any known woman who was a wife or concubine to EmperorLiu Hong, and thus, not of imperial blood. They would imply that he was just some random boy thatDong Zhuomade emperor.↩
“Previously, I andHanWenjiejointly established an everlasting moral path, with the intent to have thosewithin the Seasbehold a lord who can bring about renewal. Currently, the west claims to have ayoung lord, who, however, has no [imperial] blood relation. TheExcellencies, theMinisters, and those below such ranks all serve[Dong] Zhuoas his sycophants. How can they be trusted ever again? We need only to garrison strategic passes, then they all will decline and perish in the west. In the east, a sage lord has been established. There can be hope for supreme peace. How can you have any doubt?2With our family being slain, then if you do not consider what[Wu]Zixudid,3how can you ever look towards the north again?4To oppose Heaven brings ill fortune. I hope you will carefully think aboutthis.”•••
It seems here thatYuan Shaois making defeatingDong Zhuoappear as a very easy process and thatLiu Yuwould soon become the emperor of all of the realm, which would then makeYuan Shuappear to be holding back from supporting the plot in full for no good reason.↩
The majority of the Yuan clan was exterminated byDong Zhuo.Wu Yunlost his father and older brother to the ruler of Chu, but he was able to avenge his family by leading a highly successful military campaign against the state.Yuan Shaosuggests thatYuan Shufollow such an example.↩
Ministers faced the north when paying homage to the emperor.Yuan Shaois saying thatYuan Shucannot be considered loyal if he does not join in the plot.↩
[Yuan] Shureplied:
“TheEmperoris clever and foresighted, possessing the character of theAccomplishedKingof Zhou. The traitor[Dong] Zhuoexploited the moment of chaos, intimidating common officials to submit to him. This is thus an instance of minor peril for theHan dynasty. The chaos is still not enough to collapse [the dynasty], and I also intend to revive it. To say that the current sovereign ‘has no [imperial] blood relation’, how can that not be slander? Ever since [the time of] our forebears, successive generations [of our clan] followed each other one after another, having loyalty and justice as their priority. TheGrand Tutorwas just, benevolent, kind, and compassionate. Although he knew the traitor[Dong] Zhuowould have brought disaster and harm to him, he sacrificed himself for justice out of faithfulness, unwilling to leave. Our household has been destroyed, the dead scattered everywhere. We were blessed to receive help from those who came from afar and nearby. To not exploit this chance to suppress traitors to the nation, in a higher sense, and sweep away imperial humiliations, in a lower sense, but to instead scheme in this [moment] has never been heard before. To also say that ‘with our family being slain, how can you ever look to the north again?’, how was that done by the empire when it was[Dong] Zhuowho committed it? The command of a lord is that of Heaven. Heaven cannot be made an enemy, all the more when it was not even the command of the lord.5I am most earnest and loyal. My will is in exterminating[Dong] Zhuo. I know not of anythingelse!”•••
This is referencing a passage from theCommentary of Zuo, the relevant part stating: Huai, the brother of Xin of Yun was about to kill the King of Chu, saying, “The previous King of Chu killed our father. If I kill his son, would that not be appropriate?” Xin said, “When a lord slays his subject, who would dare make him an enemy? The command of a lord is that of Heaven. If death is by the command of Heaven, who would be the enemy?”↩
Your minister,[Pei] Songzhi, would examine this. TheRecords of Famed Heroesstated, “Chen Wen, styledYuanti, was fromRunan. He previously served as theInspectorofYang province, fell ill, and died.Yuan ShaosentYuan Yito lead the province, who was defeated and scattered, fleeing toPei commanderybefore being killed by soldiers.Yuan Shuthen usedChen Yuto serve inYang province.[Chen] Yu, styledGongwei, fromXiapi.[Chen] Yuwas controlling the province when[Yuan] Shuwas defeated atFengqiuand went south towardsShouchun.[Chen] Yuopposed[Yuan] Shuand did not admit him.[Yuan] Shuretreated to protectYinling, then gathered troops once more and attacked[Chen] Yu.[Chen] Yuwas fearful, and he fled toXiapi.” If this is so, then[Chen] Wenwas not killed by[Yuan] Shu. Thus, this is not similar to the base biography.
WhenLi JueenteredChang’an, he wished to ally with[Yuan] Shufor assistance. He appointed[Yuan] Shuas theGeneral of the Left, enfeoffed him asMarquisofYangzhai, and gave himacting tally.Grand TutorMa Midiwas sent to hence award the seal, but[Yuan] Shustole the tally of[Ma] Midiand forced him to stay, not sending himback.C
TheAnnotations to the Definite Records of the Three Capital Commanderiesstates:[Ma] Midi, styledWengshu, was the son of a kinsman ofMa Rong. When he was young, he succeeded the legacy of[Ma] Rong, and was promoted due to his talent and learning. He,Yang Biao,Lu Zhi, andCai Yong, among others, compiled and audited the central depository.6They would reach the positions of theNine Ministersand ascend the government as imperialaides.•••
In clearer terms, this passage says that these four men were responsible for reviewing and editing several books.↩
TheChronicles of the Astute Emperorstates:[Yuan] Shuborrowed the tally from[Ma] Midito look at it, then stole it and did not return it. He prepared to have over one thousand people in his army and commanded him to hurriedly recruit them.[Ma] Miditold[Yuan] Shu, “The ancestors of your noble family served asExcellencies. What do you have to say about your recruitment of soldiers?7And then when you told me to hurry that process, do you presume officials under theExcellenciescan coerce and obtain them?” He asked to leave to[Yuan] Shu, but[Yuan] Shumade him stay and did not let him go. Because he lost the tally and the humiliation of bending [toYuan Shu], he became stressed and angry, and hedied.•••
From how I interpreted the Classical Chinese text, it seemed to me thatMa Midithought that recruiting soldiers are beneath the gentry, or at leastYuan Shu. The tone of voice thatMa Midiuses is also meant to shameYuan Shu.↩
At the time, theChancellorofPeiwasChen GuiofXiapi, who was the son of the younger brother of formerGrand Commandant[Chen] Qiu.[Yuan] Shuwas of noble descent,8as was[Chen] Gui, and they had journeyed together in their youth. He wrote to[Chen] Gui,saying:•••
Here, “noble descent” refers to those whose clan had members who served asExcellencies.↩
“When theQinlost their power in erstwhile times, the herd of mighty heroes in the realm contended to seize it, but in the end, the one both wise and valorous received all their capitulations. Now that there is division and trouble in the affairs of the world, there is the situation of disorder once more. This is truly the time for the heroic and righteous to act. We are old friends. How could I ever consent to making you but a mere attendant? Once I manage all important matters, you, sir, would be my heart and backbone.”
The middle son of[Chen] Gui,[Chen] Ying, was inXiapiat the time.[Yuan] Shualso coerced and took[Chen] Yinghostage, presuming that it would surely summon[Chen] Gui.[Chen] Guireplied with a letter that stated:
“During the last years of Qin of erstwhile times, the wild and brutal did as they pleased, and tyranny overflowed the realm. There was constant harm and death to the common people, and the lowly could not bear to live. Hence, it would transpire in the division of the land. Although troubled this declining era may be, it is not yet like the excessive violence during the end of the Qin.General Cao, with his divine might, has responded to these times. He has brought back order and law, and he will pacify the violent and evil, and he will bring stability to thosewithin the Seas. This is true, and there is proof. I believe you ought to join powers with him and be of the same heart as him. This would support and uphold theHan dynasty. Hidden plots are unlawful, and by personally experimenting with disaster, how could you not be condemned? If you are lost but know your way back, you can still would avoid this. We are old acquaintances, and so I am explaining this with all my feelings. Although this may be perverse to your ears, it is affection of flesh and bone.9If you want me seek gain and fawn, then even with the crime of death Icannot!”•••
Here, “flesh and bone” is a metaphor for close intimacy. The phrase in which it appears can also be translated as “affection of the highest sort”.↩
In the second year of theera [195], winter, theSon of Heavenwas defeated atCaoyang.[Yuan] Shuconvened his subordinates and told them, “Right now, the Liu clan is becoming weaker, andwithin the Seas, there is great turmoil. My family, for four generations, wereExcellenciesand imperial aides, and the commoners look to join me. I want to respond to Heaven and obey the people. Thus, gentlemen, what are your thoughts?” Among the group, there were none who dared reply.Master of RecordsYan Xiangsaid, “Of old, theZhou, fromHoujito theCivil King, accumulated virtue and collected achievements. The realm under heaven was partitioned into three, and they had two portions thereof, yet they still obeyed and served theYin. Your enlightened lordship, though generations after generations [of your clan] have been very prosperous, that is not yet akin to the splendor of theZhou; and though theHan dynastymay be weak, that is not yet akin to the cruelty of[King] Zhou ofYin.”[Yuan] Shuwas silently displeased. He used the auspicious portents ofZhang JiongofHeneito illegitimately take the imperial title.DHe had the [title of]Grand AdministratorofJiujiang commanderybe madeIntendantofHuainan, and he installedExcellenciesandMinisters, and he offered sacrifices in the north and south suburbs.10He indulged in the most extreme of extravagances, and his rear palace had hundreds [of concubines], all donning fine silk with patterns. He had much exquisite millet and meat,Ebut his soldiers were freezing and starving. Within theJianghuai region, it was desolate, and the people had to eat eachother.•••
Emperors would sacrifice to Heaven in the north and south suburbs of a capital city. This sentence is used to describe just how brazenYuan Shuwas in asserting emperorship.↩
TheSummarized Documentsstates:[Yuan] Shupurported that the Yuan surname originated from the Chen [surname]; the Chens were descendants ofShun. He purported that earth succeeds fire,11thus responding to the sequence of destiny, and he had also read prophetic texts that said, “The successor to theHanshall be of earthly loftiness.” Because his forename and style name matched such,12he established what would be called the Zhongdomain.•••
The Five Phases [五行] indeed does describe earth as following fire.↩
The character I translated as “earthly” is 塗, but it can also mean “route” [途]. The prophecy thus can sometimes translated as “The successor to theHanshall be high on the route”. The forename ofYuan Shuis Shu [術], and one of its many definitions is “street”. The style name ofYuan ShuisGonglu[公路], which literally means “noble road”.Yuan Shuthus assumed a correlation between the vague prophecy and his own style name.↩
TheChronicles of the Nine Provincesstates: ThedaughterofFeng Fang, the[Colonel who] Directs Retainers, was a national beauty. She fled the chaos toYang province. When[Yuan] Shuascended the city and saw her, he was delighted. He thus took her in and lavished her with extreme adoration. The various women hated her favor, and told her, “TheGeneralvalues people with ambition and integrity. You should cry and act depressed when opportune. This will certainly make him respect you even more.”Lady Fengthought this was right. When she later met[Yuan] Shu, she wept tears.[Yuan] Shuindeed thought she had ambition in her heart, and cared her for more.13The various women thus strangled her together and hung her from the beam of the lavatory.[Yuan] Shugenuinely thought she, being unable to achieve her ambitions, died, and thus accorded a lavishfuneral.•••
It seems the women thought that had their plan worked,Yuan Shuwould stop favoringLady Fengbecause he would have felt annoyed, perhaps even threatened.↩
[Yuan] Shuwas first defeated byLü Bu, then he was later defeated by theGrand Progenitor. He fled to his subordinates,Lei BoandChen Lan, atQian mountain, but he was refused by them. He became anxious and fearful, and he did not know where to go. He was going to give the title ofEmperorto[Yuan] Shaoand intended to go toQing provinceto followYuan Tan, but fell ill on the road anddied.F
TheBook of Weistates: The concession of the title of Emperor from[Yuan] Shuto[Yuan] Shaostates:
“TheHanhas lost the world for too long, for theSon of Heavenis being controlled, the governance is with [powerful] family clans, people of might are competing with each other, and the lands are being torn asunder. This is no different thanZhouhaving seven divided nations during its waning years, concluding with thestrongestannexing all. Furthermore, the Yuan clan has received the destiny of being rulers, and there are auspicious omens that have indicated such. As of now, you, sir, have four provinces and millions of people and households, and there is no one who is mightier in strength and there is no one who is greater in virtue. ThoughCao Caowants to support the feeble and save the weak, how can he prolong what is dead and salvage what is destroyed?”
[Yuan] Shaosecretly agreed with this.
TheBook of Wustates: When[Yuan] Shuwas refused byLei Boand others, he remained for three days, but his soldiers ran out of supplies, so they thus returned back toJiangting, eightyliaway fromShouchun.14He inquired his chef [for food], and there was only thirtyhuof wheat flour. At the time, it was the sweltering summer. He wanted to get a drink of honey, but there was no honey. He sat on his bed, sighing for an especially long time, and then exclaimed, “Have I,Yuan Shu, come to this?” He then fell down from his bed and vomited manydouof blood beforedying.•••
Assuming that oneliwas about 416 meters, eightyliwould have been 33.28 kilometers, or 20.68 miles.↩
His wife and children relied on the former official of[Yuan] Shu, theGrand AdministratorofLujiang,Liu Xun, butSun Cedefeated[Liu] Xun, and they were seized. Thedaughterof[Yuan] Shuentered the palace ofSun Quan, and his son,[Yuan] Yao, becamePalace Gentleman. The daughter of[Yuan] Yaoalso married the son of[Sun] Quan,[Sun] Fen.
